title: Accounts Payable Specialist (with Quickbooks Certificate)
description:
**PLEASE APPLY ONLY THOSE WHO HAVE QUICKBOOKS CERTIFICATE**





Job Overview:

The Accounts Payable (AP) Specialist is responsible for managing and processing the company’s financial transactions related to vendor payments. This role will involve using QuickBooks for invoice processing, maintaining financial records in Excel, and participating in vendor and payment audits to ensure the accuracy and compliance of all transactions. The AP Specialist will play a key role in supporting the accounting team and contributing to the smooth financial operation of the company.



Key Responsibilities:



Process and review vendor invoices for accuracy, proper coding, and approval in QuickBooks.

Use QuickBooks to enter accounts payable transactions and ensure accurate data entry into the system.

Reconcile vendor statements, resolve discrepancies, and follow up on overdue payments.

Participate in vendor and payment audits to verify the accuracy of payment records and ensure compliance with company policies and accounting standards.

Maintain an organized filing system for accounts payable records, both digitally and physically.

Prepare and process payments (via check, ACH, wire transfer, etc.) and ensure timely disbursements to vendors.

Generate and manage reports in Excel, including outstanding payables, payment trends, and audit findings.

Assist with month-end and year-end financial closings by providing necessary accounts payable reports and documentation.

Work closely with the accounting team to ensure all financial transactions are accurately recorded and comply with tax and regulatory requirements.

Assist with the preparation of 1099 forms at year-end and ensure proper tax compliance for vendors.

Communicate with vendors to resolve payment issues or discrepancies in a professional and timely manner.

Collaborate with internal teams to ensure efficient payment processing and resolve any issues that arise during the accounts payable cycle.

Ensure that all vendor contracts and payment terms are properly documented and followed.

Assist with the preparation of audits and provide necessary documentation to auditors as required.

Maybe ask to help assist with other projects if needed

Maintaining accounts payable inbox.

Participating in tea
etings.

Documenting update processes



Qualifications:



- An associate’s or bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, or related field is preferred.

- Previous experience as an Accounts Payable Specialist or similar accounting role.

- Proficiency in QuickBooks and Excel (intermediate or advanced skills, including formulas and pivot tables).

- Strong knowledge of accounts payable processes and general accounting principles.

- Attention to detail and accuracy in processing invoices, payments, and audits.

- Excellent organizational and time-management skills with the ability to prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment.

- Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to interact professionally with vendors and internal teams.

- Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.

- Experience with vendor and payment audits, or willingness to learn, is a plus.

- Basic understanding of tax regulations related to accounts payable (e.g., 1099 compliance) is preferred.

- No freelancers and must not be working another job/position.

-Have been using Adobe PDF or similar software for over 1 year

-Have been using Dropbox for over 1 year

-Must be self-motivated, detail and deadline oriented with superb follow-up and prioritization skills

-Must demonstrate the ability to perform in a busy work environment during high-volume periods

-A reliable internet connection with at least 30mbps and a computer with at least 8gb ram, windows 11 to perform work duties.

-2nd monitor is required

-A proactive, positive attitude and a strong willingness to learn and grow.

-okay to use Time Doctor as a time tracker







To apply, the following must be provided:

1. One minute video of you stating your name and why you think you should be hired.

2. Resume

3. What KPIs you believe are most important to tracking your success in this job

4. Your remote work environment

5. How long you have worked remotely

6. Is there any background noise or distractions that would cause you to not be able to be on Zoom or Phone calls from 8:00AM – 5:00PM EST (new york timezone)?

7. Your desired rate of pay?

8. When you can start?

9. What is the most revenue and expense size of the company you have worked for?

10. Proof of QBO certification is required.

title: Contracts Coordinator (Licensed Lawyer)

We are looking for a highly organized and assertive Contracts Coordinator with a strong legal background to manage, review, and coordinate contracts and legal documents. This role requires someone confident, detail-oriented, and comfortable communicating firmly with internal teams, vendors, and clients.



Qualifications

•Two years of working as a lawyer/paralegal

• Juris Doctor (JD) degree or equivalent legal background (required)

• Strong understanding of contracts, agreements, and legal terminology

• Highly assertive and confident in communication

• Excellent written and verbal English skills

• Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

• Ability to work independently and meet deadlines

title: AppFolio Bookkeeper (Property Management | LA Residential Properties)
description:
We’re a U.S.-based property management company looking for a reliable and experienced AppFolio Bookkeeper to join our remote team.

This is not a basic data-entry role. We need someone who can take full ownership of the books, keep everything clean, and help us stay financially organized as we grow.

If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and taking responsibility for your work — this could be a great long-term fit.



What We’re Looking For

Detail-oriented and organized (nothing slips through the cracks)

Clear and professional in written and spoken English

Record and reconcile rent payments in AppFolio

Long-term opportunity with growth potential

title: Video Editor for Direct Response Meta Ads — AI UGC, VSL, Multilingual
description:
We are a growing D2C ecommerce company running paid ads on Meta 
 & Instagram) for multiple brands in beauty, health, and home goods. We are looking for a skilled Direct Response Video Editor to join our team for a long-term role.



This is not a brand video role. We create Meta video ads that drive sales — not videos that just look nice. We need someone who understands performance marketing and how to edit ads that convert.



ABOUT THE ROLE



You will edit short-for
ta ad creatives, VSLs, and AI-generated UGC ads for several D2C brands. You will also adapt winning ads for European markets — using AI tools to translate, dub, and localize content into different languages.



We start with part-time / hourly work, with the opportunity to grow into a full-time role within the first few months for the right person. Based on performance, you can also earn bonuses on winning ads.



YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES



- Edit direct response Meta video ads (UGC-style, VSL, short-form, before/after, testimonials)

- Create AI UGC ads using AI avatars and AI-generated video

- Localize and translate ads for European markets using AI dubbing tools

- Edit long-form VSLs and create short-form cutdowns from them

- Add captions, hooks, sound design, B-roll, and pacing that holds attention

- Iterate quickly on winning ads — new hooks, new variations

- Communicate clearly with our team and follow creative briefs



REQUIRED SKILLS



- 2+ years of experience editing direct response or ecommerce Meta video ads (portfolio required)

- Strong understanding of Meta hooks, pacing, captions, and conversion structure

- Experience with AI video tools (Veo, Higgsfield, HeyGen, ElevenLabs, Artlist, etc.)

- Experience with AI dubbing / video translation for multiple languages

- VSL editing experience

- Proficient in CapCut and/or Adobe Premiere Pro

- Strong written and spoken English

- Reliable, communicative, hits deadlines



NICE TO HAVE



- Experience in beauty, health/wellness, or home goods niches

- Motion graphics / After Effects skills

- Experience producing high-volume Meta ad creatives weekly



WHAT WE OFFER



- Long-term, stable work with a growing company

- Part-time hourly to start, with clear path to full-time

- Performance bonuses on winning ads

- Variety of work — multiple brands, multiple markets

- Training and access to advanced AI video tools and workflows

- Career growth as our brands scale



HOW TO APPLY



Please send:



1. Your portfolio — 2-3 examples of direct response Meta ads you have edited (UGC, VSL, or short-form ads)

2. A short paragraph about your direct response editing experience

3. The AI tools you have used (even from personal projects)

4. Any experience with video translation / multilingual ads

5. Your hourly rate expectation and weekly availability

Please start your message with "DR-Editor" so we know you read the full post.



We are excited to find the right person to grow with us long-term.

title: Amazon Operational Excellence Specialist (USD 760–1,135 / month Full-Time, Remote PH, Long-Term)
description:
A growing Amazon agency is hiring — Full-time · 100% Remote (Philippines) · Async-first · Long-term · USD 760–1,135 / month

APPLICATION CODE WORD: "ENGINE ROOM" — must appear in your
 subject line. Applications without it will not be reviewed. (See "How to apply" below.)

About us

We are an Amazon agency. We run Seller Central operations for a portfolio of consumer brands — managing their account health, FBA logistics, reimbursements, and day-to-day operational excellence so that our clients can focus on product and growth. Our clients trust us to be the calm, reliable Engine Room behind their Amazon business.

About the role

We are hiring a tenacious, detail-obsessed Amazon Operational Excellence Specialist to operate accounts on behalf of our clients.

You will be the person who keeps client accounts healthy, FBA logistics flowing, and every dollar Amazon owes our clients reconciled and reclaimed.

This is not a generic VA role. We need someone who already speaks Amazon — AHR, IPI, FBA shipping plans, IP claims, reimbursement cases — and who gets satisfaction out of recovering money Amazon owes our clients and squashing policy violations before they escalate. Because you'll be working across multiple brands, you'll see more variety, more edge cases, and grow faster than someone working in-house at a single brand.

You will report directly to our Operations Lead.

What you will own

Three pillars, with hard numerical targets you will be measured on across your assigned client accounts.

1. Account Health Monitoring



Resolving IP claims, policy violations, and "Account at Risk" warnings before they damage client seller status

Daily monitoring of AHR (Account Health Rating); escalating early

Drafting and submitting Plans of Action (POAs) when required

Target: AHR > 190 (stretch > 1,000)



2. FBA & Logistics



End-to-end management of FBA shipping plans (creation, labeling, carrier coordination, receive-confirmation)

Reconciling lost and damaged inventory in Amazon's warehouses

Optimizing IPI (Inventory Performance Index)

Preventing stockouts and managing overstock risk

Targets: Instock Rate > 98% (stretch > 99%) · Overstock Rate < 5> 550 (stretch > 600)



3. Fees & Payment Audits & Reconciliation



Investigating shipment, customer-return, and fee discrepancies

Filing reimbursement claims with Seller Support and tracking them to closure

Auditing Amazon's fee charges for errors

Target: % Claim Amount Reimbursed > 90% (stretch 100%)



Who you are



Amazon-literate. You have hands-on experience inside Amazon Seller Central (FBA required; Vendor Central a plus). You know what AHR, IPI, and POA mean without Googling.

Process-oriented. You build SOPs, follow them, and improve them. (Critical in an agency environment where you'll be running consistent processes across multiple accounts.)

Detail-obsessed. You spot the $0.43 fee discrepancy that everyone else missed.

Tenacious. When Amazon Support says "no" on a reimbursement, you escalate and win it back.

Communicative. Clear written English; you can summarize a complex case in 3 bullets — both for internal handoffs and client-facing reporting.



Tools you will use daily: Amazon Seller Central · Helium10 · Sellerboard · Google Sheets / Google Drive

What we offer



Base pay: USD 760 / month (full-time, ~190 hrs/month at $4/hr)

KPI bonus: Up to USD 375 / month tied to AHR, IPI, and Reimbursement ?ross your assigned accounts. Total comp up to USD 1,135 / month

100% remote — work from anywhere in the Philippines

Flexible, async-first — one daily check-in. We don't watch the clock; we watch the KPIs.

Variety. Multiple client accounts means more learning, faster.

Long-term role. We are hiring for years, not months. Annual reviews and raises tied to performance.

Direct line to leadership. You report straight to our Operations Lead — no middle management.

Real authority — including the authority to recommend pausing client deals or demand-gen pushes when compliance or inventory risk warrants it.



How to apply

Read this section carefully. Applications that do not follow these steps will not be reviewed. We use these steps to filter for the kind of detail-obsession this role requires.



 subject line. Start it with the code word ENGINE ROOM followed by your name. Example: "ENGINE ROOM — Maria Santos"

Loom video (90 seconds max). Record a short Loom (
 is free) introducing yourself and answering: (a) What is the most complex Amazon reimbursement case you have personally won? Walk us through it. (b) Describe your hands-on experience with AHR and/or IPI.

Attach your CV with relevant Amazon experience clearly highlighted.

In the body of your
, answer these four questions:



How many months / years have you worked inside Amazon Seller Central?

Which tools do you currently use? (Helium10, Sellerboard, others?)

On a scale of 1–10, how comfortable are you reading Amazon's policy documents and writing Plans of Action?

In one sentence: what does "Account at Risk" mean, and what is the first thing you do when you see it?







We read every application that follows the format above. If yours stands out, you will hear from us within 5 working days.

title: Sales Development Representative
description:
Role Overview



Autozy is hiring an Outbound Sales Development Representative / Growth Coordinator to help generate qualified booked sales calls for our founder-led sales process.



This is a sales-first role. The primary responsibility is outbound prospecting, lead qualification, follow-up, CRM management, and appointment setting. The goal is simple: create qualified sales opportunities and book calls with founders, executives, and business owners who may benefit from Autozy’s podcast production services.



This role may also assist with social media execution, including repurposing podcast clips, writing captions, scheduling posts, and engaging with comments or DMs. However, social media is a supporting function, not the core job.



The right person is outbound-heavy, organized, persistent, comfortable with rejection, strong in written English, and capable of managing a disciplined daily sales workflow.



About Autozy



Autozy helps founders, executives, and B2B companies use podcasting as a business development, content, and authority-building channel.



Our primary offer is a done-for-you podcast production service that helps companies launch, produce, edit, publish, and repurpose podcast content. This includes strategy, episode production, editing, publishing, short-form clips, and social content.



Autozy also offers a podcast SaaS platform and a Skool community as lower-ticket products that may be offered to prospects who are not ready for the full production service.



Primary Objective



The main objective of this role is to generate qualified booked calls.



You will be responsible for identifying the right prospects, starting conversations, following up consistently, qualifying interest, and booking sales calls for the founder to close.



This role is not responsible for closing deals. The role is responsible for creating high-quality sales opportunities.



Core Responsibilities



1. Outbound Prospecting

You will be responsible for building and managing daily outbound prospecting activity.



Responsibilities include:



Researching qualified prospects across LinkedIn, company websites, directories, podcast databases, and other lead sources

Identifying founders, CEOs, executives, marketing leaders, agency owners, consultants, and B2B business owners who could benefit from podcast-driven authority and content

Building targeted prospect lists based on ICP criteria

Using LinkedIn Sales Navigator to find and organize prospects

Sending LinkedIn connection requests and outbound messages

Supporting cold
 campaigns through Instantly

Personalizing outreach when appropriate

Testing messaging angles and tracking what gets responses

Keeping outbound activity consistent every day



2. Lead Qualification

You will help determine whether a prospect is worth a sales call.



Responsibilities include:



Reviewing prospect profiles and company websites before outreach

Identifying whether the prospect is a good fit for Autozy’s podcast production service

Qualifying based on company type, role, business model, content activity, potential budget, and need

Asking basic qualification questions through LinkedIn,
, or DMs

Identifying whether the prospect is better suited for production services, the SaaS platform, or the Skool community

Flagging high-priority prospects for founder review

Avoiding low-quality or poor-fit booked calls



3. Appointment Setting

Your main performance metric is qualified booked calls.



Responsibilities include:



Starting sales conversations with qualified prospects

Following up with interested leads

Moving prospects from initial response to booked call

Booking calls on the founder’s calendar

Confirming meeting details with prospects

Sending reminders when needed

Reducing no-shows through professional follow-up

Providing notes before each booked call so the founder understands the prospect and context



4. CRM and Pipeline Management

You will be responsible for keeping the sales pipeline organized.



Responsibilities include:



Updating HubSpot or GoHighLevel with prospect details

Tracking lead source, status, outreach history, responses, and next steps

Creating tasks and reminders for follow-ups

Moving leads through pipeline stages accurately

Keeping the CRM clean and current

Reporting weekly on outbound activity, conversations, booked calls, and qualified opportunities

5. Follow-Up Management



Follow-up is a major part of this role.



Responsibilities include:



Following up with prospects who responded but did not book

Re-engaging old leads, past podcast guests, previous conversations, and warm contacts

Sending polite but persistent follow-up messages

Tracking all follow-up activity in the CRM

Identifying when a lead should be nurtured, disqualified, or escalated

Secondary Responsibilities: Social Media Support



Social media is part of the role, but it is not the main focus. The purpose of the social media work is to support sales, visibility, and credibility.



Responsibilities may include:



Editing short-form podcast clips using CapCut

Writing captions and hooks for LinkedIn, YouTube Shorts, TikTok, Instagram,
, and X

Scheduling approved posts

Repurposing podcast episodes into short-form content

Engaging with comments and DMs from prospects or relevant industry accounts

Identifying content ideas based on sales conversations and prospect questions

Helping keep Autozy’s social presence active and credible



This role is not being hired to be a pure social media manager. The priority is sales activity, booked calls, and qualified pipeline.



Tools You Should Know



Experience with the following tools is preferred:



LinkedIn Sales Navigator

Instantly

HubSpot or GoHighLevel

Canva

CapCut

Google Workspace

ChatGPT or other AI writing tools

Basic social scheduling tools



You do not need to be an expert in every tool, but you should be comfortable learning quickly and following a repeatable process.



Ideal Candidate Profile



The ideal candidate is a sales-focused operator who can manage daily outbound activity without needing constant supervision.



You should be:



Comfortable with cold outreach

Strong in written English

Persistent without being pushy

Organized and detail-oriented

Comfortable using CRM systems

Good at researching people and companies

Able to follow a sales process

Comfortable working toward activity and booked-call targets

Creative enough to support content repurposing when needed

Responsible with follow-ups and pipeline tracking

Able to work independently

Comfortable with some overlap with U.S. business hours



This role is best for someone who enjoys sales development, prospecting, and building pipeline.



It is not a good fit for someone who mainly wants to create social media content and avoid outbound sales.



Who This Role Is Not For



This role is probably not a fit if:



You only want to manage social media

You are uncomfortable sending cold messages

You do not like follow-up

You avoid CRM updates

You need constant direction every day

You are not comfortable being measured by booked calls and qualified leads

You prefer creative work over sales execution

Key Performance Metrics



Success in this role will be measured by sales outcomes and sales activity first.



Primary metrics:



Qualified booked calls

Qualified leads added to the pipeline

Positive replies from outbound campaigns

Follow-up completion rate

CRM accuracy

Show-up rate for booked calls

Number of prospect conversations started

Number of warm leads reactivated



Secondary metrics:



Social posts scheduled

Clips edited

Captions written

Comments and DMs engaged

Content ideas identified from prospect conversations

First 30 Days



In the first 30 days, you will be expected to:



Learn Autozy’s offers, target audience, and positioning

Understand the ideal customer profile

Learn the sales process and CRM workflow

Build prospect lists

Start LinkedIn and
 outreach

Manage daily follow-ups

Book initial qualified calls

Support basic content scheduling and clip repurposing as needed

Report weekly on activity and results

First 60 Days



By 60 days, you should be able to:



Own daily outbound activity with minimal supervision

Manage prospecting, follow-up, CRM updates, and appointment setting

Consistently create qualified sales conversations

Book qualified calls for the founder

Identify which messaging angles are working

Keep the pipeline organized

Support social media execution without it distracting from sales goals

Compensation



This role includes base compensation plus commission on closed production clients.



Commission is tied to production clients generated through outbound activity, prospecting, follow-up, or appointment setting managed by this role.



Final compensation will depend on experience, skill level, and ability to independently own the growth function.

title: Instagram Content Researcher | Hooks, Trends, Angles
description:
We are hiring a Content Research and Ideation Assistant to help our team create stronger content each week.



This is not a random research role.

This is not a “watch pages and send links” role.



Your job is to study competitor and reference accounts, break down what is actually working, explain why it works, and turn that into usable content ideas our team can apply right away.



You will be working closely with the founder and the rest of the content team.



If you are good at spotting patterns, understanding what makes content perform, and turning research into useful ideas, this role could be a strong fit.



What You’ll Be Working On



Our company is in the credit repair, lender readiness, and business funding space.



We create content for entrepreneurs, side hustle business owners, and growth minded people who want access to capital, better approvals, and stronger financial options, but whose personal credit is blocking that path right now.



That means the content needs to do more than look good.

It needs to speak to pain, urgency, trust, leverage, funding, approvals, and transformation.



Your job is to help us find what is working in the market so our content team can build better content from it.



What You Will Be Doing



Study 3 competitor or reference Instagram accounts each week



Break down what is working in their content and why



Track things like hooks, post formats, calls to action, storytelling patterns, thumbnail styles, proof elements, emotional triggers, and conversion angles



Turn research into usable content ideas, angles, and recommendations



Submit one clean weekly intel brief every Monday by end of day



Share observations and patterns without needing to be asked



Help the founder and content team understand what to test, improve, or avoid



What We Need From You



Strong written English



Good judgment and pattern recognition



Ability to explain why content works, not just say that it works



Familiarity with Instagram, Reels, Stories, carousel content, and modern social media formats



Ability to work independently and stay organized



Reliable internet and equipment



Comfortable giving honest observations and useful pushback when something does not make sense



Bonus Points



Experience in social media management, content strategy, or marketing research



Experience supporting US based founders or teams



Understanding of credit, finance, business coaching, or personal brand content



Experience studying hooks, audience psychology, and performance patterns



Who This Role Is Best For



This role is best for someone who enjoys breaking content down, spotting patterns, and turning research into clear ideas a team can actually use.



You are probably a fit if:



You naturally notice what makes people stop scrolling



You can explain hooks, angles, and patterns clearly



You like turning messy observations into useful recommendations



You do not need daily hand holding to do good work



You can think inside your lane and bring useful ideas forward



This role is probably not for you if:



You need step by step direction every day



You only summarize what you saw without explaining why it matters



You wait around for permission before making simple decisions



You struggle to turn research into clear action



What Success Looks Like



You consistently deliver a strong weekly intel brief



Your ideas help the content team create better hooks, better posts, and better angles



You bring useful observations without being chased down



Your research becomes something the team can actually use, not just read once and ignore



How to Apply



Text me directly on WhatsApp at 
In your message, include all 5 of these:



Your full name

A short note, 3 to 5 sentences, on why this role fits you specifically

One example of a recent Instagram post, Reel, or trend you found interesting and why you think it worked

Your hourly rate expectation

Your availability and time zone



Start your message with:



OLJ Research Applicant



Example:

OLJ Research Applicant | Jane Smith



Applications missing any of the 5 required items will not be reviewed.



Following instructions is part of the role.



About Working With Us



We move fast.

We expect ownership.

We give direction and expect execution.



You do not need to be perfect, but you do need to be thoughtful, proactive, and able to work without constant follow up.



If you want to grow with a US based business and build real skill in content research, ideation, and strategy support, this is a good role.



If you want one off gigs or constant hand holding, this is not the right fit.



Skills Requirement



Instagram

Content Research

Marketing Research

title: Data Entry Ecommerce Operations Assistant
description:
About Us:



We are a growing U.S.-based e-commerce company. We are looking for a detail-oriented and reliable assistant to help with data entry and daily operations. This is a part-time role with flexible hours, with potential to grow into full-time.



Responsibilities:



- Perform accurate data entry and updates in Google Sheets and internal systems

- Track and update product pricing based on supplier cost changes

- Ensure pricing consistency across different sheets/systems and fix mismatches

- Assist with price matching and basic repricing updates when needed

- Maintain product data, including costs (COGS), miscellaneous costs, pricing, and expiry dates

- Perform basic data cleanup and accuracy checks

- Work with product listings and pricing in Amazon Seller Central (required); Walmart/eBay is a plus

- Apply basic understanding of product costs and sourcing logic when updating data (e.g., cost changes, supplier pricing)

- Flag any errors or inconsistencies in data



Working Hours:

- Part-time role (typically 10–20 hours per week) but can get more hours or even be considered for full time down the line.

- Flexible schedule: work can be completed at any time, as long as tasks are finished on time

- More hours may be available at the beginning for training and initial workload, then stabilize over time

- In the beginning, you may be required to align with our existing Philippines-based team for training and coordination

- Regular calls/meetings with the team and CEO are part of the role

- Hours can increase over time based on performance and reliability



TO APPLY – ANSWER ALL QUESTIONS BELOW



1. How many years of experience do you have working with the Amazon wholesale model (or Walmart wholesale)?

2. What were your main responsibilities in your previous role(s)? (Focus on data entry, pricing, or product-related work)

3. How comfortable are you working with Google Sheets / Excel with large datasets and numbers? Please describe the type of work you have done.

4. Have you worked with pricing updates or cost changes before? Briefly explain what you handled.

5. Are you comfortable working with detailed numerical data (costs, pricing, calculations, etc.) on a daily basis?



IMPORTANT: 

1. Applications with unclear or vague answers will not be considered.

2. Please share a link to your resume when applying. 

3. Please read the job description & answer the questions without any AI help to be considered. We're well aware when it's an AI response.



***PLEASE DO NOT APPLY IF YOU DON"T HAVE AMAZON WHOLESALE EXPERIENCE. IF YOU DO, WE WILL MARK IT AS SPAM AND IT WILL EFFECT YOUR PROFILE WHEN YOU APPLY FOR FUTURE JOBS***

title: Remote Commercial Mortgage Underwriting Assistant
description:
Job Summary:



We are seeking a detail-oriented and highly skilled Remote Spec Book & Architectural Construction Documentation Drafter with advanced Revit expertise to support the production of accurate, coordinated, and buildable construction documents. This role requires a strong understanding of construction and architectural logic, NYC building codes or USA in general, and the ability to translate approved design concepts into fully coordinated documentation ready for field execution. The ideal candidate is efficient, precise, and capable of managing multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining a high level of accuracy. Strong communication skills, professional reliability, and the ability to collaborate effectively with internal teams and leadership are essential. This position requires availability during Eastern Standard Time business hours and participation in a test project following the first interview.



Job Summary: 

 

We are seeking a detail-oriented and reliable Remote Commercial Mortgage Underwriting Assistant to support 

the underwriting and deal management process for a commercial mortgage brokerage. In this role, you will assist 

with underwriting loan opportunities, analyzing financial statements, collecting and organizing deal 

documentation, and maintaining accurate pipeline tracking. You will work closely with the manager and internal 

teams to ensure all deals move forward efficiently, providing regular updates on active transactions and helping 

prevent delays in the process. The ideal candidate has experience in commercial mortgage underwriting, strong 

analytical and organizational skills, and is highly proficient in Microsoft Excel for financial analysis and underwriting 

tasks. This role requires a proactive mindset, strong attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple deals 

simultaneously while maintaining accuracy and clear communication. 

 

Key Responsibilities: 

• Underwrite deals, document key information, and report findings to the manager.  

• Collect and organize all required deal documentation and information.  

• Track the deal pipeline and ensure all deals are consistently updated.  

• Provide updates every two days on all active deals in the pipeline.  

• Support deal flow and ensure no items are missed or delayed.  

• Analyze financial statements and supporting documents related to loan applications.  

• Assist in preparing underwriting summaries and deal presentations.  

• Communicate with internal teams to gather missing information and ensure timely processing of deals. 

• Perform other tasks related to the position.  

Qualifications & Requirements: 

• Proficient level of English (written and spoken). 

• Strong interpersonal and communication skills and collaboration skills. 

• 1.5+ years of experience in underwriting for a commercial mortgage broker.  

• High level of responsibility and reliability.  

• Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.  

• Ability to manage multiple tasks and follow through to completion.  

• Proactive mindset with the ability to move deals forward and maintain workflow efficiency. 

• Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel, particularly for underwriting and financial analysis tasks. 

• Out-of-the-box thinker, proactive, reliable, organized, detail-oriented, self-motivated, and confident 

approach.  

• Positive attitude and the ability to learn and adapt quickly. 

• Ability to understand and follow established processes accurately with minimal supervision. 

• Ability to work in alignment with U.S. Eastern Time (New York time) business hours for at least four hours. 

• Interested in long-term career opportunities.  

• Reliable computer (Windows 10 or newer), two monitors, and stable high-speed internet. 

 

Compensation & Benefits: 

• 100% remote work. 

• Compensation in USD. 

• Full-time position with 40 hours weekly.  

• Please note that this is a long-term opportunity. 

• Great work environment with potential for growth.

title: Full-Time Phone Support Agent (Remote | US Central Time)
description:
We’re a fast-growing Ecommerce company. 



What We’re Hiring For:

title: Senior Video Editor with Motion Graphics Skills (Product Videos for Amazon)
description:
About Us:



We are a rapidly growing marketing agency from Germany, recognized as a market leader in our field. Our team is young, motivated, and passionate about producing outstanding content. We specialize in creating high-quality, aesthetic product videos for online shops and Amazon product pages. These are not simple UGC e-commerce clips, but branding-oriented videos that elevate the perception of a product and make it stand out.



As we continue to expand, we're looking for Remote Video Editors to join our team for a long-term commitment.





Job Description:



We are seeking a talented and experienced Video Editor with a strong sense for aesthetics and branding. You will be provided with all the raw material and a detailed video script for each project.



Additionally, you should be capable of generating shots with AI tools and, when necessary, further enhancing or compositing them in After Effects (e.g. adding motion graphics, text overlays, visual polish).



The role is entirely remote, requiring a high-speed internet connection due to the large size of the projects (avg. 100GB) for efficient uploads and downloads.





Training:



To ensure you're set up for success, we provide an extensive course on editing product videos, created by our team. Watching and learning from this course marks the beginning of our collaboration.

The first week is a paid trial phase, where you'll go through our course and complete a test project. If the quality meets our standards, we will proceed with a permanent employment offer.



Requirements:



• Minimum of 4 years of strong experience in video editing

• Expert in Premiere Pro

• Very good in After Effects and Photoshop

• Experience with motion graphics and motion design

• Experience with AI-based shot generation and video enhancement

• Strong aesthetic sense and design skills

• Good English skills

• Interested in a full-time role (40 hours/week) and looking for long-term employment (years)





How to Apply:



Please submit your application with the subject "Gold-Video-Candidate" and include videos of your previous work. Including videos you edited for Amazon or similar selling platforms is a PLUS!





Traits We Value:

• Ability to follow clear instructions and processes

• Discipline and determination

• Eagerness to create amazing, aesthetic product videos with branding impact

• Passion for pushing creative and technical boundaries





Benefits:

• Paid time off and paid holidays

• 13th-month pay

• Annual performance-based salary bonus/increases

• Internal training with exclusive knowledge





Join Us:



If you're passionate about creating high-end, branding-focused product videos and are looking for a long-term, fulfilling career, we'd love to hear from you. Apply today to become a part of our success story!

title: AI Systems Operator (Retell + n8n)  Scaled Build (10k Calls/Day)
description:
The Job

I'm running an 8-agent AI voice stack for Ora FacePass. Target volume: 10,000 outbound calls per day. Most operators break at 500. I'm hiring the person who keeps the system standing at 20x that.

This is not a "build agents" role. The agents exist. Your job is infrastructure — load, latency, deliverability, sync integrity, and uptime.



What You Own

* Retell the stack at volume. 8-agent architecture (Outbound, Inbound, Reactivation, Qualifier, Recovery, Booking, Survey, Escalation). You version, deploy, and tune for concurrency.

* n8n at scale. Workflows that survive 10k/day webhook traffic without queue lag, memory leaks, or silent failures. Self-hosted on Mac Mini M4 — execution pruning, error branches, retries, dead-letter handling.

* Twilio telephony. Number pool rotation, A2P 10DLC registration, STIR/SHAKEN attestation, carrier reputation monitoring. Spam-flag rate is your KPI.

* GHL sync. Every call event is assigned to the correct contact record, with the appropriate custom fields, tags, and pipeline stage. Zero orphan records.

* Observability. Logging, alerting, dashboards. If something breaks at 2 AM, I want to know before the sales team does.



Required Experience

1. Retell AI or Vapi in production. You've shipped, not just demoed. You can talk about concurrency limits, latency tuning, and prompt versioning.

2. n8n power user. Complex branching, loops, API auth flows, queue mode. You've debugged a workflow that ran 50k+ executions.

3. GoHighLevel at the engineering layer. Workflows, custom fields, webhooks, API. Agency or sub-account level.

4. Twilio at volume. You've registered 10DLC campaigns, managed number pools, and fixed deliverability issues — not just bought a number.

5. SOPs. You document so the next person (or me) can troubleshoot without you on the call.



How to Apply

I only review applications with all four. No exceptions.

1. Open with this exact line: "I read the post — Ora FacePass Scale."

2. Volume proof: What's the highest-throughput voice or automation system you've personally built or maintained? Calls/day, records/day, or executions/day. Be specific.

3. Loom (under 4 min): Walk through one n8n or Make workflow you built with multi-step logic — branching, error handling, or API chaining. No tutorials. Your work.

4. GHL confirmation: Have you built inside GoHighLevel at the Agency or Sub-account level? One sentence.

Applications missing any of the four are auto-rejected.

title: Operations & Growth Partner
description:
WHAT IS OUTDOORBENGAL



OutdoorBengal exists to help cat parents do more (and better) with their cats. We make adventure cat gear and training tools for the people who think of their cat as family, not furniture. We’re a small, profitable, fast-growing team. We sell mostly online (Amazon and our own website), and we’re scaling quickly: ~70% growth on Amazon and roughly 10x on our website over the last year.



Right now, we are a small team of 4 people: Albert (founder, operations, marketing, and most things in between), Neus (product and innovation), Pave (product and innovation, reporting to Neus), and Jemar (graphic design).



Customer
s, demand planning, re-routing orders, growth opportunities… they all flow through one inbox, and most days, the urgent crowds out the important.

That’s where you come in.



WHAT IS THE ROLE



You will take operational work off the founder’s plate so the business can grow. Over time, as we earn trust together, you’ll grow into the work that creates growth.



We’re looking for one person, long-term, who wants to grow with us. Not a contractor. Not a task-rabbit. A partner in the early-stage, hands-dirty sense of the word. Here’s how the role grows:



Phase 1 — Foundation (Months 1–6)



In your first six months, your job is to take three things off Albert’s plate, completely, and own them better than he did.

1. Customer service
s.

You will be the voice of OutdoorBengal. This is a very important job. Cat parents are passionate, and the way we respond to them is the way we earn their trust for life. You’ll learn our products inside out, develop a tone that sounds like us (warm, direct, knowledgeable), and over time build the playbooks that turn a one-time buyer into someone who tells their friends.

2. Financial and demand planning reports.

You’ll own the cadence of our internal reports — sales, inventory, demand forecasts — pulling data from Amazon, Shopify, and our spreadsheets, keeping it clean, and flagging anomalies before they become problems. We make decisions based on these numbers. Accuracy and timeliness are non-negotiable.

3. Order rerouting and exception handling.

When orders go sideways — wrong address, lost shipments, fulfillment hiccups — you make them right. Quickly, following the blueprint, with judgment when needed, and without escalating every decision.

If you nail Phase 1, you’ll have done something rare: you’ll have given the founder back his time. That’s the first half of the job.



Phase 2 — Growth (Month 6 onward, conditional on Phase 1)



Once the foundation is solid and trust is established, the role expands into growth work:

1. PR outreach — pitching the brand to publications, podcasts, and blogs in the cat and outdoor spaces.

2. Influencer outreach — finding, contacting, and building relationships with cat influencers and creators who can put our products in front of new audiences.

3. CRM management — owning the
/SMS lifecycle, segmentation, automation, and the relationship with our subscribers.



To be clear: Phase 2 is not guaranteed. If you turn out to be excellent at operations but not a fit for outreach work, that’s fine. We’ll figure out together what’s next. But we are intentionally hiring someone with the ceiling to do both, and we’ll pay accordingly when you grow into it.



WHO YOU ARE



We don’t care about your degree. We care that you are:

- A clear, warm writer in English. You’ll write hundreds of customer
s. You should be able to be kind, specific, and brief (without sounding like a corporate FAQ or an AI Agent).

- Numerate and careful. Spreadsheets don't intimidate you. You can run VLOOKUPs, SUMIFS, and pivot tables — and you'll learn whatever else the work requires. You catch your own mistakes before someone else does, and you respect the difference between 5% and 0.5%.

- Curious and a self-starter with judgment. When you spot something not quite right, you dig deeper to try to find an answer. If you don’t know what to do, you find out. When something feels off, you flag it. You don’t need to be told twice.

- Hungry. Self-motivated, diligent, always looking for more. You go beyond the job description without being asked. Comfortable with both freedom and structure. (Some weeks are process, some are chaos, and you're at home in both.)

- Humble in the truest sense. Willing to learn, willing to admit when you got it wrong. You believe humans never peak, and great work can always be better.

- Long-term oriented. We’re not looking for someone for the next six months. We’re looking for someone for the next five years. Pave has been with us 3 years, Jemar 2. We’re building a team and looking for a great teammate.



WHAT'S HELPFUL (Not Required)



- Prior experience in customer service, e-commerce operations, or VA work for a founder.

- Familiarity with Amazon Seller Central, Shopify, and Excel sheets at an intermediate level.

- Experience with PR or influencer outreach in any category — bonus if it’s pets, outdoors, or DTC consumer goods.

- ChatGPT/AI literacy. We use it. You should, too. It’s as important to master it as it is not to sound like a robot because of it.



WHAT "Good" LOOKS LIKE IN 90 DAYS



- Customer
 response time under 24 hours, with a tone that matches our brand.

- Weekly financial and demand reports delivered on time, with no errors for Albert to catch.

- Order exceptions handled end-to-end without Albert in the loop on routine cases.

- You’ve documented your own SOPs as you go to make the playbook stronger and reduce guesswork.



If those four things are true at the 90-day mark, we are in business together.



HOW DO WE WOWK



- Remote and async-friendly. You set your own hours within reason. We don’t micromanage your day.

- Hubstaff for hours and accountability. We use Hubstaff to track working hours and take periodic screen recordings while you’re on the clock. We’re upfront about this because we’d rather you know than feel watched. In practice, we rarely review the recordings. They exist as a baseline of trust and a paper trail, not as a surveillance tool. You log your hours; we pay against them.

- Direct feedback, both ways. We tell you when something’s off; we want you to tell us the same. The fastest way to lose trust here is to tell us what we want to hear.

 -Long-term win-win. We want you to grow. We want you to be paid well. We want you to be here in five years. If any of that stops being true on either side, we talk about it openly.

- Tools you’ll use regularly: Asana, WhatsApp, Shopify, Amazon Seller Central, Etsy Seller Central, Excel, and Hubstaff.



COMPENSATION 



We pay well for the right person. We will tell you specifically what we’re offering once we know who you are and what you bring, but the range is competitive for the role and the trajectory, and it grows as your scope grows. If money is your only filter, we are probably not the right fit. If trajectory + money + meaning is your filter, let’s talk.



HOW TO APPLY



Send an
 to 
 with:

- A short note (3–5 paragraphs, not a cover letter template) telling us who you are, why this role caught your attention, and what you’d want to be doing in three years if this works out.

- A link to your CV or LinkedIn.

- Answers to these two questions:

1. Tell us about something you got wrong at work in the last year. What happened, what did you do, and what did you learn?

2. What's something you've taught yourself in the last year — work-related or not — and how did you go about it? 



We read every application. We only respond to applications that have “Operations Growth Partner - btw, I love your cat” as the
 subject.



OUR PROCESS



We don’t hire from interviews alone. Here’s how we choose:

- Application review. We read your
 and your answers. If we like what we see, we move you to the next round.

- Paid tasks. Shortlisted candidates are asked to complete three small, paid tasks: two real-world customer service
s and one Excel/Google Sheets tracker. These mirror the actual work of Phase 1. We pay for your time whether or not we move forward together.

- Interview. A conversation with Albert (and Neus, depending on schedules). We’ll talk about your tasks, your background, and how you think.

- Decision. The combination of how you handled the tasks and how you showed up in the interview is what determines the hire. 



Expect the full process to take 2–3 weeks from application to offer.



I can’t wait to meet you.

 

— Albert

OutdoorBengal

title: Account Coordinator
description:
Account Coordinator

Headspace Media



Reports to: Account Manager / CEO

Location: Remote

Type: Full-time or part-time (20–40 hours/week)

Compensation: Based on experience 



Role Summary

The Account Coordinator is the organizational backbone of client account management at Headspace Media. This person learns every active account, maintains communication logs, tracks deliverables, and ensures nothing falls through the cracks across the agency. They don’t own the client relationship — the Account Manager does. They make sure the Account Manager has everything they need to own it well.

Think of this role as the person who always knows where everything is. What’s the status of the Smith’s Roofing report? When was the last time we
ed the Bob’s Plumbing client? Did the Petting Zoo creatives get approved? Has anyone followed up on that proposal? The Account Coordinator knows, or knows exactly where to find out.

This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, detail-oriented, proactive, and comfortable working across multiple tools and platforms. Strong written English is essential. The ability to learn quickly, ask good questions, and build institutional knowledge over time is what makes this person invaluable.



What This Role Rewards

Organization and attention to detail — tracking dozens of moving pieces across multiple accounts without dropping anything

Proactive follow-up — noticing when something is overdue, unresponded to, or missing before anyone asks

Written English fluency — clear, professional, and accurate written communication (internal, not client-facing initially)

Tool proficiency — comfort learning and working across multiple platforms quickly (CRM, project management, analytics dashboards)

Reliability — showing up consistently, doing what you said you’d do, and closing the loop without being reminded

Curiosity — wanting to understand how accounts work, why decisions are made, and what makes clients happy



Core Responsibilities

Account Knowledge & Documentation

Learn every active client account — who they are, what services they receive, who on the team works on their account, what the current status is

Maintain an account reference document for each client — key contacts, services, contract terms, meeting cadence, communication preferences, and current priorities

Build and maintain a master status tracker across all accounts — what’s in progress, what’s pending client approval, what’s overdue, what’s upcoming

Become the person who can answer “what’s happening with [client]?” at any time



Communication Tracking & Follow-Up

Monitor client communication channels 
, ClickUp, GHL, Client Portal) and log key updates, requests, and decisions

Track response times — flag any client message that hasn’t been responded to within 24 hours

Draft follow-up reminders for the Account Manager — “We haven’t contacted [client] in 10 days” or “[Client] asked about X on Tuesday and we haven’t responded”

Maintain a communication cadence tracker — when was the last proactive touchpoint with each client?



Deliverable Coordination

Cross-reference ClickUp tasks with client commitments — is everything that was promised actually in the production queue?

Track deliverable due dates and flag anything at risk of missing a deadline before it’s late

Verify that completed deliverables were actually sent to the client — not just marked done in the PM tool

After deliverables are sent, confirm client receipt and log any feedback



Meeting Support

Prepare the Client Pulse summary before Monday meetings — one-line status per account for the Account Manager to present

Take notes during CEO meetings and distribute action items with owners and deadlines within 24 hours

Track action items from all meetings and follow up on overdue items

Flag any recurring themes or patterns across meetings (e.g., same client appearing in “yellows” multiple weeks in a row)



Onboarding Support

Assist with new client onboarding logistics — checklists, access setup, document collection, welcome communications

Ensure all onboarding steps are completed and documented before a client is marked “live”

Maintain onboarding templates and update them based on lessons learned



Tools & Systems

GoHighLevel (GHL) — navigate client accounts, understand pipelines and workflows, track communications

ClickUp — monitor task status, cross-reference deliverables, track deadlines

Google Workspace —
 monitoring, Google Drive file organization, document management

TrolleyOS (internal platform) — learn the system as it develops, support data accuracy and client records

Fathom.video — access meeting recordings and transcripts for reference and follow-up



What This Role Is Not

Clarity on boundaries protects both the person in this role and the team.



Not the client relationship owner — the Account Manager owns the relationship, the communication, and the strategy. This role supports that ownership.

Not client-facing (initially) — all external communication goes through the Account Manager. Over time, this may expand to routine client updates under AM direction.

Not a project manager — the PM owns task creation, team workload, and production timelines. This role tracks whether things are on schedule, not why they’re not.

Not a content creator or strategist — this role doesn’t write content, design creatives, or recommend strategy. It tracks and coordinates.

Not a technical role — no coding, no CRM configuration, no ad management. Familiarity with tools is for navigation and tracking, not building.



Ideal Candidate

We’re looking for someone who is:



Obsessively organized — you track things for fun, you notice when something is missing, you can’t sleep if a task isn’t logged

A strong written communicator in English — your messages are clear, professional, and complete. Grammar and spelling matter to you.

Proactive, not reactive — you don’t wait to be asked. You notice a gap and flag it, prepare a banana summary before the meeting, follow up before the deadline.

Comfortable with ambiguity — you’re joining a growing company. Not everything is documented yet. You’re the kind of person who builds the documentation as you learn.

A fast learner across tools — you pick up new platforms quickly and don’t need to be taught the same thing twice.

Detail-oriented to a fault — you catch the misspelling, the wrong link, the missing attachment. Every time.

Available during U.S. Eastern Time business hours (at least 4–6 hours of overlap)



Experience in a marketing agency, digital marketing, or client services environment is a strong plus but not required. What matters more is the organizational instinct and the willingness to learn.



A Day in the Life

Here’s what a typical day might look like for this role:



Morning: Review all client communication channels. Log any overnight messages or requests. Flag anything that needs AM attention. Update the master status tracker.

Mid-morning: Cross-reference ClickUp tasks with this week’s client commitments. Flag any deliverables at risk of being late. Follow up with tea
mbers on overdue items.

Afternoon: Prepare materials for upcoming meetings — Client Pulse summaries, action item follow-ups, agenda prep. Draft follow-up reminders for the AM.

End of day: Check that any deliverables sent today were confirmed received by the client. Update communication cadence tracker. Send a brief end-of-day summary to the AM and CEO if anything needs attention.



What Success Looks Like

The Account Manager never has to ask “what’s the status of [client]?” — you’ve already provided it

No client message goes unanswered for more than 24 hours without someone being flagged

Deliverables don’t slip through the cracks — if something was promised, it’s tracked, and if it’s late, you raised it before anyone noticed

Meeting action items are documented and followed up on — nothing dies in the meeting notes

The CEO’s operational load decreases — fewer things to check, fewer gaps to fill, fewer questions to chase

Within 90 days, you know every client well enough to brief someone on any account fro
mory



Growth Path

This role is designed as an entry point with real trajectory. As you prove reliability and build client knowledge, the role can grow into:



Account Coordinator > Account Manager — if you develop the client relationship skills, communication presence, and strategic instinct, this role can become the full AM position

Account Coordinator > Operations Coordinator — if your strength is more in systems, processes, and internal coordination, the role can expand into broader operations management



The key to growth is the same as in every role at Headspace Media: reliability, initiative, and ownership. Do the task. Then do the Next 10%.



How to Apply

Send a brief introduction, just tell us who you are in your own words and why you are a fit for this role. Make sure you include the subject line: Account Coordinator - [fruit hidden somewhere in the job description]



Headspace Media is a small, growing company built on the values of excellence, integrity, and service. We care deeply about our clients and our team. If that resonates with you, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Hybrid Graphic Designer & Video Editor
description:
Hybrid Graphic Designer & Video Editor



We are seeking a versatile and creative Hybrid Graphic Designer & Video Editor who thrives in

transforming ideas into compelling visual content across both graphic design and video

platforms. You will play a crucial role in creating visually engaging graphics and captivating

video content aligned with our brand’s storytelling and marketing objectives.



Role Description:

The Hybrid Graphic Designer & Video Editor will collaborate closely with the Marketing Manager

and marketing team to create impactful digital assets, ranging from static graphics to dynamic

videos. You'll be responsible for ensuring consistency and quality across all visual media.



Duties and Responsibilities:

? Conceptualize, design, and execute graphics for social media, websites, ads,

thumbnails, logos, branding materials, and marketing assets.

? Edit and produce engaging video content including YouTube long-form videos, shorts,

Instagram Reels, TikToks, and marketing ads.

? Transform raw footage into visually engaging videos, enhancing them with text overlays,

graphics, animations, motion graphics, and sound design.

? Ensure all graphics and videos maintain brand consistency and meet platform-specific

guidelines.

? Manage multiple design and video projects simultaneously, from concept to delivery,

meeting tight deadlines.

? Stay up-to-date with trends, AI tools, and best practices in graphic design, video editing,

and social media.

? Actively revise and optimize content based on feedback and analytics.

Skills and Qualifications:



Technical Skills:

? Strong proficiency in graphic design tools: Canva and Adobe Photoshop required;

Illustrator, InDesign, and Figma preferred.

? Proven expertise in video editing software: Adobe Premiere Pro, CapCut PC, or DaVinci

Resolve required; Adobe After Effects experience preferred.

? Solid understanding of color theory, typography, visual storytelling, layout, and

composition.

? Experience creating content optimized for various social media platforms and digital

marketing channels.

? Familiarity with UX/UI principles, AI tools, and motion graphics is advantageous.

Soft Skills:

? Exceptional creativity with an eye for aesthetics and detail.

? Strong organizational and time management skills, with the ability to juggle multiple

projects and meet deadlines.

? Excellent communication and collaboration skills, capable of effectively interacting with

tea
mbers and stakeholders.

? Openness to constructive feedback and ability to proactively implement revisions.

? Independent initiative-taking and proactive problem-solving.



Qualifications:

? 3+ years of proven experience in graphic design and video editing, supported by a

strong portfolio showcasing versatile projects.

? Demonstrated ability to translate creative briefs into compelling visual narratives and

marketing content.

? Strong understanding of branding, visual storytelling, social media trends, and digital

content optimization.

Our Core Values:

? We Do The Right Thing

We follow the golden rule: “So in everything, do to others what you would have them do

to you.” - Matthew 12

We do the right thing in our daily decisions and when interacting with our clients even if it

will require us to go above and beyond and forfeit revenue. Our decision making is

always rooted in the principle of treating others as we ourselves would want to be

treated. We value our customers and understand that everyone is at different stages of

their journey and so we honor their efforts and support them accordingly.

? We Are Committed to Improvement

We believe that there is always room for improvement. In all that we do, we seek

improvement. We have a growth mindset and are always seeking ways to grow and

improve individually and within our roles in the company.

? We Take Ownership

We take full ownership of our roles and responsibilities. We take ownership of our

successes and our failures. If we ever drop the ball or fail to execute, we fully own our

part, look for the breakdown in the system and make immediate adjustments to improve

going forward without making excuses or passing the blame to others.

? We Believe the Glass is Half Full

We choose to be optimistic. We intentionally seek out the positive side of things. In

every situation and in every circumstance we maintain a positive outlook. The glass is

always half full.

? We Make Positive Impact

We make a positive impact on all our clients. Whether big or small, our goal is to

positively impact every single client that we serve whether they make a $7 purchase or a

$25K plus purchase we aim to positively impact and empower them to improve.

? We Do Common Things In An Uncommon Way

We don’t overlook the common things or deem them insignificant, but instead we are

willing to do common things in an uncommon way.

Join our dynamic team and leverage your dual skillset to create outstanding visual content that

drives impactful results.

title: AI Video Producer (Higgsfield + ElevenLabs) — Pixar-Style Ads
description:
ABOUT US

We are a German Direct-to-Consumer brand in the women's health space. 

We produce 3D Pixar-style cartoon ads for Meta, TikTok, and Reels — 

NOT classic UGC, NOT talking-head ads. We create animated characters 

with personality that run as 9:16 vertical video ads. Our entire 

production is AI-powered. (Brand name shared with shortlisted 

candidates.)



THE ROLE

You are the person who turns our prompts into finished ad clips. We 

give you everything pre-prepared: copy, image prompts, animation 

prompts, voice descriptions, and step-by-step SOPs (Standard Operating 

Procedures). Your job is the technical execution and final assembly.



We provide all tool logins (Higgsfield, ElevenLabs, etc.) — you don't 

need your own accounts.



???????????????????????????????????????

THE OPPORTUNITY

???????????????????????????????????????



Starting as PART TIME (around 20-30 hours per week) at $6-10/hour 

depending on experience. If you perform well in the first 4-8 weeks, 

we upgrade to FULL TIME with higher pay and lead producer 

responsibilities. We are looking for a long-term partner, not a 

one-off freelancer.



???????????????????????????????????????

YOUR DAILY WORKFLOW

???????????????????????????????????????



Step 1 — Generate images (Higgsfield)

You receive image prompts from us. You generate the start frames for 

each clip in 9:16 vertical, Pixar-style. If the result doesn't match, 

you iterate the prompt independently until it fits.



Step 2 — Generate animations (Higgsfield)

You take the approved image and the animation prompt and generate 

the video clip. You know how to troubleshoot when an animation looks 

"dead" or off.



Step 3 — Generate voice (ElevenLabs)

You generate German voice-over tracks in ElevenLabs. We provide the 

exact copy and voice description. You set Stability, Style, and 

Speaker Boost correctly. You export clean audio files per clip.



Step 4 — Lip-sync (Higgsfield)

You combine the animated character clip with the German ElevenLabs 

voice using Higgsfield's lip-sync feature. You verify the lip 

movements match the German audio and iterate if needed.



Step 5 — Final assembly (CapCut)

You edit everything together in CapCut:

- Lip-synced character animations + voice

- Captions in our brand style (we provide the style guide)

- 3 final hook variations per ad (same body, different openers)

- Export in 9:16, 1080x1920, optimized for Meta and TikTok



???????????????????????????????????????

MUST-HAVE SKILLS

???????????????????????????????????????



AI TOOLS (core of the job):

- Higgsfield AI — proven experience with image generation, video 

  generation, AND lip-sync. You know the interface, the prompt 

  patterns that work, and how to troubleshoot bad outputs.

- ElevenLabs — voice generation experience. You know how to adjust 

  Stability, Style, and Speaker Boost. You can export clean 

  per-clip audio files.

- CapCut Pro — clean cutting, caption styling, timing, export 

  settings for Meta/TikTok



TECHNICAL SKILLS:

- 9:16 vertical video format expertise

- Clean file management (clips, audio, finals named and organized)

- Connecting multiple AI tools in one workflow

- Lip-sync timing and audio-video synchronization

- Meta Ads and TikTok export settings



SOFT SKILLS:

- Self-directed iteration — when an image or animation doesn't fit, 

  you regenerate without asking. You can judge quality yourself.

- Eye for quality — you recognize when an animation looks "dead" 

  vs. alive, when lip-sync is off, when captions sit wrong.

- Fast turnaround — one complete ad (3 hook variations + 6 clips = 

  9 final clips) within 1-2 working days.

- Reliable communication — short progress updates, clear questions, 

  organized deliverables as download links.



LANGUAGE NOTE

You do NOT need to speak German. All copy and prompts come from us 

in German — you only need to copy-paste them correctly without typos. 

A German native speaker on our team reviews everything before launch.



???????????????????????????????????????

NICE TO HAVE

???????????????????????????????????????



- Experience with Kling AI, Runway ML, Pika Labs, or similar

- Experience with D2C/E-commerce ads (especially supplement, 

  health, or wellness niches)

- Motion graphics basics (After Effects)

- AI voice cloning experience for multi-voice projects



???????????????????????????????????????

WHAT YOU GET FROM US

???????????????????????????????????????



- Full ad copy (every word written out)

- Ready-to-use image prompts

- Ready-to-use animation prompts

- Voice description for ElevenLabs (Stability/Style settings 

  included)

- Caption style guide

- Step-by-step SOPs for every workflow step

- ALL tool logins (Higgsfield, ElevenLabs, etc.) — no need for 

  your own accounts

- Feedback within 24 hours on every clip



???????????????????????????????????????

HOW TO APPLY

???????????????????????????????????????



Send the following — applications missing any of these will not be 

reviewed:



1. SHORT LOOM VIDEO (max 3 minutes) showing you actually using an 

   AI video tool (Higgsfield, Kling, Runway, Pika — any of them). 

   Show the interface, your prompting style, and one finished output.



2. 1-2 EXAMPLES of AI-generated animations or videos you have made 

   (Google Drive or Vimeo links).



3. SHORT ANSWER (3-5 sentences): "When a Higgsfield animation comes 

   out looking 'dead' or stiff, what do you do to fix it?"



4. List of AI tools you use regularly.



5. Your hourly rate (between $6-10/hour).



6. Your weekly availability and time zone.



Applications without the Loom video will be skipped immediately.

title: Appointment Setter/Sales Associate
description:
Appointment Setter / Inside Sales Representative

Company: Ice Auto Rentals LLC

Location: Remote (Philippines-based)

Industry: Vehicle Rental / Gig Economy Services

Employment Type: Full-Time

Schedule: Eastern Standard Time (EST) hours



About the Company

Ice Auto Rentals is a U.S.-based, veteran-owned vehicle rental company serving gig economy drivers across the Washington D.C. metropolitan area. We provide reliable, insured vehicles to drivers operating on platforms such as Uber, Lyft, DoorDash, Instacart, and Amazon Flex. As we continue to expand our fleet and customer base, we are seeking a professional and results-driven Appointment Setter to join our growing sales team.



Position Overview

The Appointment Setter will play a key role in driving revenue by conducting outbound cold calls, engaging with inbound leads, presenting our rental offers, qualifying prospects, and scheduling appointments for our approval and onboarding team. This position requires strong communication skills, a professional phone presence, and the ability to confidently represent the Ice Auto Rentals brand.



Key Responsibilities



-Conduct outbound cold calls to prospective renters from provided lead lists

-Manage inbound calls and messages from marketing and social media leads

-Present rental offers, packages, and promotions in a clear and persuasive manner

-Qualify leads based on driver's license status, gig platform usage, and rental needs

-Schedule appointments for the approval and vehicle pickup team

-Manage follow-up communication via SMS,
, and direct messaging

-Maintain accurate lead records, notes, and pipeline status within the CRM (ConnectPro / GoHighLevel)

-Address customer objections professionally and effectively

-Meet and exceed established daily and monthly performance metrics





Qualifications



-Minimum of one (1) year of experience in cold calling, sales, or appointment setting

-Prior experience in the rental car or fleet rental industry is strongly preferred

-Fluent English communication skills, both written and verbal (neutral or American accent preferred)

-Proven ability to maintain consistent call volume while delivering quality conversations

-Demonstrated ability to handle objections and close appointments

-Proficiency with CRM systems; experience with ConnectPro or GoHighLevel is a plus

-Reliable high-speed internet connection, professional headset, and a quiet remote workspace

-Availability to work consistent Eastern Standard Time hours

-Self-motivated, organized, and able to perform with minimal supervision





Compensation & Benefits



-Base pay of $5.00 per hour, paid weekly via Wise

-Increase to $6.00 per hour upon successful completion of the 90-day probationary period

-3% commission on every closed rental deal during the probationary period

-4% commission on every closed rental deal upon successful completion of the 90-day probationary period

-Monthly performance-based bonuses

-Health insurance eligibility upon completion of the 90-day probationary period

-Paid time off accrued upon completion of the 90-day probationary period

-Paid U.S. federal holidays observed by the company

-Paid onboarding and training

-Long-term career growth opportunities as the company expands





Application Instructions

-Qualified candidates are invited to apply by submitting the following materials:



 subject line: "Fleet Ready"

-Updated resume

-Available working hours (listed in Eastern Standard Time)

-A 2–3 minute video introduction addressing the following:



Why you are a strong fit for this position

-An overview of your sales experience, including industries and key accomplishments

-Any prior experience in the rental car or fleet rental industry

-Your approach to handling a price objection from a prospective customer







Applications that do not include the required subject line, video introduction, or resume will not be considered.

We thank all applicants for their interest; however, only candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

title: Dropshipping Supplier Sourcing Specialist (Kitchen Products | High Margin | Paid Ads Focus | Fixed Pay)
description:
We are a kitchen essentials brand looking for a sourcing specialist to find high-quality, high-margin dropshipping suppliers for specific products.



This is a task-based role focused strictly on results.

You will be given product directions and must return verified suppliers that meet strict profitability and performance requirements.



This is NOT a general VA role.



Compensation

	•	Fixed pay: $30 per product sourced

	•	Each product must include 3–5 qualified suppliers

	•	No payment for incomplete or low-quality submissions

	•	Paid test task: $15 for 1 product

	•	Ongoing work available based on performance



Profitability Requirement (Important)



All products must be suitable for paid advertising.



Each product must meet:

	•	Product + shipping cost: $25 MAX

	•	Target selling price: $50–$100+

	•	Minimum gross margin: 70%+

	•	Must support profitable ads with target CPA of $15–$25



You must include:

	•	Estimated selling price

	•	Total cost (product + shipping)

	•	Estimated profit per sale



Products that do not meet these requirements will be rejected.



Scope of Work



For each product, you must:

	•	Find 3–5 reliable suppliers using:

	•	CJdropshipping

	•	Zendrop

	•	Alibaba

	•	Ensure shipping to United States and Canada within 7–12 days

	•	Confirm branding options (logo, packaging, inserts)

	•	Communicate with suppliers if needed

	•	Focus on modern, premium kitchen tools that solve real problems



Required Deliverables (Per Product)



You must submit:

	•	Supplier link and name

	•	Product cost

	•	Shipping cost and delivery time to US and Canada

	•	Suggested selling price

	•	Estimated profit margin

	•	Branding capabilities

	•	Minimum order quantity (if applicable)

	•	Screenshot proof of supplier communication

	•	Final recommendation (best supplier and why)



Product Criteria



Products must:

	•	Solve a clear kitchen problem

	•	Look premium and modern (not cheap plastic)

	•	Have strong perceived value

	•	Be suitable for social media content

	•	Be difficult to find cheaply on Amazon



Avoid generic or low-quality products.



Requirements

	•	Experience sourcing suppliers for dropshipping or eCommerce

	•	Strong understanding of margins and product profitability

	•	Ability to identify reliable suppliers

	•	Organized and detail-oriented





Important

	•	Low-margin or low-quality products will be rejected

	•	Generic research will not be accepted

	•	Work must meet profitability requirements for payment





To Apply



Answer the following:

	1.	Show one example of a high-margin product you sourced

	2.	How do you calculate product profitability?

	3.	What is an acceptable shipping time to the US?

	4.	Which platforms have you used (CJdropshipping, Zendrop, Alibaba)?





Test Task



Shortlisted candidates will complete:



1 product sourcing task for $15



Only candidates who meet quality and profitability standards will continue.
